metadata.analyst_note: All figures sourced from SP_Analysis BOD files. No estimates or guesses.
data_sources: Daily Cash Fcst - 11.14.23_BU view_adUPDATE_11.14.23_BUPNL_HW_Assurant Nov gross_NovBOD.xlsx
Daily Cash Fcst - 1.21.25_BU view_deck version_working_Apr append_deck2_JanBOD.xlsx

business_structure.summary: Three distinct home warranty/protection streams: (1) Assurant-underwritten HW distribution through SHS, (2) Cinch B2B service fulfillment where Sears handles Cinch customers' service calls, (3) Hello Super - Sears' own branded HW product launched mid-2023.

assurant_partnership.description: Sears Home Services (SHS) distributed Assurant-underwritten home warranty products. SHS collected gross premiums from customers, remitted ~75% to Assurant as the insurer/underwriter, kept ~25% as revenue. Contract sales peaked Apr-Jun 2023 (~56-60K contracts/month) then STOPPED in Jul 2023 as new contract sales ceased. Residual payments continue on legacy in-force contracts. Assurant also pays SHS a quarterly profit-sharing payment based on combined portfolio performance.

pct_to_assurant.note: Assurant's share of gross premiums. SHS retained ~24-31%.

cinch_b2b.description: Cross Country Home Services (CCHS, rebranded Cinch) is a separate entity that PAYS Sears to handle service calls for Cinch's own home warranty customers. This is a B2B service fulfillment contract. Payments were ~$4.7-5.6M/month in FY22, declining to ~$0.6-1.4M/month by 2026.
cinch_b2b.note: CCHS/Cinch B2B inflows are separate from the Cinch HW outflows in the disbursements section. The disbursements labeled 'Cinch HW' appear to be reserves/claims-related costs associated with SHS acting as servicer.

hello_super.description: SHS launched its own branded home warranty product 'Hello Super' starting Jul 2023, replacing the Assurant-distributed product. SHS retains the full economics but also bears the claim risk. Monthly costs ~$0.15-0.58M growing to ~$0.53-0.58M by 2025-2026.

assurant_of_fcst_key_data.note: From 'Assurant OF fcst' tab in Aug 2023 and Nov 2023 files. Shows the ramp-down of new contract sales.

Jul_2023.note: Last month with new contract sales. After Jul 2023: new sales = 0

Aug_2023_onward.gross_premium_M: Declining tail of in-force contracts only
Aug_2023_onward.note: New contract sales ceased. Wind-down of existing portfolio only.

implied_active_contracts_peak.note: Peak active contracts estimated from premium data

nov_2023_bupnl_hw_assurant_gross.key_finding: November 2023 shows a $7.583-7.585M Assurant payment. The 'Nov gross' in the file name indicates this is the GROSS Assurant premium payment for November - a catch-up from the Sep/Oct zero payment months.

context.interpretation: The Sep-Oct-Dec zero months with a large Nov payment reflects timing of Assurant invoice processing. The 'gross' label confirms this is before any netting with profit-share or claims adjustments.
context.assurant_OF_fcst_context: The 'Assurant OF fcst' tab confirms outflows timing: 60-day lag on invoice remittance. The Nov 2023 payment likely covers Aug-Sep 2023 invoice period based on 60-day lag.

jan_2025_assurant_fudge.finding: The term 'Assurant fudge' appears in the file name but not as a labeled tab or cell within the spreadsheet. Investigation shows the 'fudge' most likely refers to the October 2024 catch-up payment.

evidence.interpretation: The $3.806M October 2024 payment is ~3-7x the typical run-rate, suggesting an accumulated true-up or reconciliation of Assurant invoices not paid in prior months (May, Aug, Sep 2024 all showed $0 payments).
evidence.catch_up_math: May + Aug + Sep implied ~$0.8-1.0M each = ~$2.4-3.0M accumulated unpaid, plus a possible contractual true-up = $3.806M
evidence.profit_share_context: First Assurant Profit Sharing inflow appeared Apr 2024 ($5.1M), suggesting a formal reconciliation of the entire Assurant relationship was done around that time. The 'fudge' may refer to the manual adjustment needed to true-up the Assurant balance after the profit-share agreement was settled.

assurant_profit_sharing_inflows_quarterl.note: These are INFLOWS from Assurant to SHS, representing profit sharing on the legacy portfolio. Declining as the portfolio runs off.

home_services_assurant_outflows.note: Net cash disbursements from SHS to Assurant for home warranty premiums. Negative = outflow from SHS. Source: BU beta 'Home Services Assurant' row, Mar 2026 file.

cinch_hw_outflows.note: Cash disbursements labeled 'Cinch (Home Warranty)' in BU beta. Represents SHS costs related to Cinch HW service fulfillment. Negative = outflow. Source: Mar 2026 BU beta.

hello_super_hw_outflows.note: Cash disbursements labeled 'HelloSuper (Home Warranty)' in BU beta. SHS's own HW product costs. Source: Mar 2026 BU beta.

assurant_profit_sharing_inflows.note: INFLOWS from Assurant to SHS. Quarterly payments, declining as legacy portfolio runs off. Source: BU beta 'SHS Assurant Profit Sharing' row.
assurant_profit_sharing_inflows.note_FY25: FY25 total lower because profit share schedule shifted to smaller quarterly amounts as portfolio winds down

cchs_cinch_b2b_inflows.note: Cross Country Home Services (Cinch) pays SHS for service call handling. These are INFLOWS to SHS. Source: BU beta 'Cross Country Home Services' inflow row and Inflows Detail tab.
cchs_cinch_b2b_inflows.trend: Declining from ~$5.5M/month FY22 to ~$0.7M/month by Apr 2026, then zero. Cinch winding down its service agreement with SHS.

hw_reserves_and_deposits.note: Cash flows to/from restricted HW reserve accounts. Source: BU beta 'HW Reserves & Deposits' row.

hw_reserves_balance_M.note: Balance of Home Warranty reserves and deposits in Trapped Cash tabs. Includes Protect Co restricted cash and service contract reserves.
hw_reserves_balance_M.cash_file_details_note: Cash file details.xlsx confirms: HW Reserves outflow '$-1.66M in YTD actuals based on premiums received (%) from service contracts'. FY25 total: $-2.66M. FY26 forecast: $-2.7M/year.

annual_net_hw_cash_position_M.note: Net cash impact of all HW-related flows. Negative = net cash drain on SHS/Company.

FY22.note: Large net negative because Assurant outflows include the full premium remittance (SHS retained ~25% as revenue but it came through gross inflows separately)

FY23.note: FY23 was near-breakeven due to still-large CCHS inflows offsetting Assurant outflows

FY24.note: FY24 turned positive due to large Assurant profit sharing inflows ($14.76M) offsetting declining CCHS inflows and growing Cinch/HelloSuper costs

FY25.note: FY25 turned negative as profit sharing declined sharply and Cinch/HelloSuper costs grew. CCHS inflows are estimated.

is_assurant_net_revenue_or_cost.answer: MIXED - Assurant was a net COST when viewed as premium outflows alone, but becomes net positive when Assurant profit sharing is included.

detail.conclusion: The gross premium outflows represent SHS remitting the insurer's share (~75%) of premiums collected. The SHS revenue retention (~25%) appears in HS inflows, not shown here. When the quarterly profit-share back from Assurant is included, the NET impact of the Assurant partnership is POSITIVE in FY24 ($+3.88M) as the legacy portfolio runs off and Assurant distributes accumulated profits. However, the original business model had SHS retaining only ~25% gross margin with Assurant taking 75%.

active_contracts_implied_by_cash_flows.rationale: By Jan 2024, Assurant invoices were ~$1.14M/month vs. ~$6.2M at peak. At same unit economics (~$110/month average invoice per contract), this implies ~10,000 remaining active contracts.

what_happened_after_post_reductions_Mar_.answer: The March 2026 'post-reductions' event did NOT end the Cinch or HelloSuper programs. Both continue but at scaled-back levels.

assurant_post_reductions.status: Wind-down continuing at ~$0.3-0.6M/month (tail of legacy portfolio)
assurant_post_reductions.outlook: Approaching zero as legacy contracts expire

cinch_post_reductions.trend: Relatively stable, suggesting ongoing B2B service fulfillment commitment

hello_super_post_reductions.trend: Growing slowly, suggesting HelloSuper portfolio still accumulating

cchs_b2b_post_reductions.May_2026_onward: Forecasted to reach zero - Cinch ending service agreement

hw_reserves_post_reductions.note: Reserves remain held for claims on in-force HelloSuper/HW contracts

what_happened_after_post_reductions_Mar_.summary: Post-reductions did not terminate HW programs. It appears to have been a headcount/cost reduction that affected other areas of the business, not the HW partnerships specifically. The trajectory shows: Assurant nearly done, CCHS/Cinch B2B ending, HelloSuper and Cinch service costs continuing at ~$1.7M/month combined.

risk_management_insurance_context.note: RiskMgt/Ins in the disbursements section is SEPARATE from Assurant home warranty. It covers D&O, Property, Workers Comp, GL, Auto insurance.
risk_management_insurance_context.monthly_range_M: $(1.0-6.4)M per month (variable due to premium payment timing)
risk_management_insurance_context.note2: Do not confuse with Assurant HW - these are Sears' own insurance programs

restricted_cash_hw.account_10218_note: Account 10218 referenced in context is for 'Penske Funded Controlled Disbursement' (found in Balance Sheet Cash tab). NOT the HW restricted cash.
restricted_cash_hw.hw_restricted_cash: Tracked as 'Home Warranty reserves and deposits' in Trapped Cash tab, not via specific account 10218.

service_contract_restricted_cash.note: Service contract restricted cash (Transform) - separate from HW reserves
